title: Halwa Puri Breakfast

seo.title: Authentic Halwa Puri Recipe: Pakistani Breakfast Delight
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Halwa Puri, a beloved Pakistani breakfast. This easy recipe features sweet semolina halwa, fluffy puris, and savory chana masala. Perfect for a weekend treat!
seo.h1: Authentic Halwa Puri Recipe: A Pakistani Breakfast Delight
h2Headings: Step-by-Step: How to Make Halwa Puri
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant plate of authentic Halwa Puri, featuring sweet semolina halwa, golden fried puris, and savory chickpea curry.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the taste of Pakistan with this homemade Halwa Puri breakfast, a delightful combination of sweet and savory.

description: Halwa Puri is a quintessential Pakistani breakfast, offering a delightful contrast of sweet and savory flavors. This beloved meal features fluffy, deep-fried puris, a rich and sweet semolina halwa, often accompanied by a spicy chickpea curry (chana masala). It is a festive and comforting treat, perfect for weekends or special occasions with family and friends.

instructions.title: Prepare the Puri Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine all-purpose flour, semolina, salt, and sugar. Add 2 tablespoons of oil and mix well with your fingertips. Gradually add lukewarm water, kneading until you form a smooth, pliable dough. It should be firm but soft. Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for at least 30 minutes to an hour.

instructions.title: Start the Halwa Preparation
instructions.text: While the puri dough rests, begin making the halwa. In a separate saucepan, heat the ghee over medium heat. Add the semolina and green cardamom pods. Roast the semolina, stirring continuously, until it turns light golden brown and releases a fragrant aroma. Be careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Make the Halwa Syrup
instructions.text: In another small pot, combine water and sugar for the halwa syrup. Bring it to a boil, stirring until the sugar dissolves completely. You can add a pinch of orange food color at this stage if desired for a vibrant hue. Keep the syrup hot.

instructions.title: Cook the Halwa
instructions.text: Carefully pour the hot sugar syrup into the roasted semolina, stirring constantly to prevent lumps. The mixture will bubble vigorously. Reduce the heat to low and continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until the halwa thickens, absorbs all the liquid, and starts to separate from the sides of the pan. This process will take about 5-7 minutes. Cover and let it sit for a few minutes off the heat.

instructions.title: Shape the Puris
instructions.text: Divide the rested puri dough into small, equal-sized balls, about the size of a golf ball. Lightly grease your hands and a clean surface with oil. Roll each dough ball into a thin, even circle, approximately 4-5 inches in diameter. Ensure they are not too thick, as this will prevent them from puffing up properly.

instructions.title: Fry the Puris and Serve
instructions.text: Heat the remaining oil in a deep kadai or frying pan over medium-high heat until it's very hot (a small piece of dough should sizzle and rise immediately). Carefully slide one puri into the hot oil. Gently press it down with a slotted spoon to help it puff up. Once puffed and golden brown on one side, flip it and fry the other side until golden. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on paper towels to drain excess oil. Repeat with the remaining puris. Serve the hot puris immediately with the sweet semolina halwa and a side of savory chana masala.

tips: For perfectly puffed puris, ensure your oil is very hot before frying and gently press the puri down in the oil with a slotted spoon.
Using fine semolina (sooji) for both the puris and halwa yields a smoother texture. For the puri dough, adding a little semolina gives them a crispier edge.
Always serve Halwa Puri fresh and hot. Puris tend to lose their crispness quickly. You can prepare the halwa and chana masala in advance, reheating them before serving with freshly fried puris.
article: Halwa Puri Breakfast is a beloved Pakistani Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
